what_it_does: Acceptance test for the X13 R2 semantic ranking term in Convergence. It exercises Suite methods for R2 scoring, gathering, capping, context resolution, and chat context with deterministic embeddings stubs to verify that semantic relevance ranks context, embedder failure degrades safely with a warning, weight zero preserves the pre-X13 ranking, and the operator message can thread through as intent.
observations: Uses deterministic vector stubs to simulate embedder UP and DOWN without calling a live model.
Splits checks into config constant existence, PRESERVE, EMBEDDER UP, EMBEDDER DOWN, and INTEGRATION parts.
Squeezes R2_BUDGET so only one gathered item can survive the cap.
Detects embedder-down warnings by scanning store.recent_events for kind=warning and embed/semantic in summary.
Asserts that resolved context blocks contain the literal string CONTEXT RESOLVED AT YOUR LOCUS.
Uses a shared ui://chrome/inbox locus and two annotations with equal recency and proximity.
standouts: The embedder-down case is treated as the critical degrade-with-warning fallback.
Deterministic orthogonal vectors make cosine(intent, relevant) high and cosine(intent, irrelevant) zero.
Semantic weight zero is proven to leave the pre-X13 recency/proximity/pin ordering unchanged.
conventions: Large module-level docstring maps each test part to a named spec case.
Uses a global PASS counter driven by a check(label, cond) helper.
Restores the original fabric.client.complete_embeddings after each stubbed call.
Tests private Suite methods directly as seams.
concerns: Tightly couples the test to private method signatures and internal string output.
Warning detection relies on brittle summary text matching rather than a structured event field.
Budget math assumes text lengths and a small fixed padding determine a single survivor.
Integration test calls _chat_context twice with overlapping responsibilities.
notes: A spec-driven acceptance test that proves R2 semantic ranking works, fails safely, and preserves the old ranking when disabled.
questions: Where is R2_SEMANTIC_WEIGHT defined and what is its default value?
What exact event structure does Suite emit when the embedder is unreachable?
How does _r2_score_and_cap accept and propagate the intent parameter?
What produces the CONTEXT RESOLVED AT YOUR LOCUS marker in the resolved block?
How are raw annotations rewrapped into the bracketed text form seen by _r2_gather?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it tests UP, DOWN, PRESERVE, and INTEGRATION cases for the X13 semantic ranking term.
apparent_themes: Degrade-with-warning rather than silent failure or crash.
Deterministic model mocking for acceptance testing.
Backward compatibility via a zero-weight fallback.
Relevance-aware context ranking for operator intent.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish that adding a semantic term to R2 context ranking is safe, observable, and recoverable, and that the operator chat message can serve as the relevance query.
novelty: It uses hand-crafted orthogonal unit vectors to deterministically simulate high-vs-zero relevance, and it makes embedder failure an explicit first-class acceptance scenario rather than an afterthought.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance harness that encodes the expected semantic-ranking behaviors and embedder-failure fallback, including reusable stub embeddings and explicit assertions about ranking, capping, and warning emission.
summary_for_embedding: company/tests/conv_semantic_rank_acceptance.py is an acceptance test for the X13 R2 semantic ranking term that uses deterministic embedding stubs to verify relevance-based ranking, embedder-down fallback with warning, and backward-compatible zero-weight behavior.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to prove that R2 context ranking can incorporate operator-intent semantics while remaining safe and backward-compatible when the embedder is unavailable or disabled.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter on success.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition and, on success, increments the module-level PASS counter and prints an ok line. It is the single verification primitive used throughout the acceptance test.

symbols.does: Deterministic stand-in for the fabric embedder when it is reachable.
symbols.description: _stub_embeddings_up(transport, inputs, model, **kw) maps each input string to a fixed unit-ish vector so that cosine(intent, relevant text) is high and cosine(intent, irrelevant text) is zero, simulating a working embedder without network calls.

symbols.does: Deterministic stand-in for the fabric embedder when it is unreachable.
symbols.description: _stub_embeddings_down(transport, inputs, model, **kw) raises RuntimeError with an embed-endpoint-unreachable message, letting the test verify that the semantic term degrades to zero with a warning instead of crashing.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two numeric vectors.
symbols.description: _cosine(a, b) calculates the dot product of a and b, divides by the product of their Euclidean norms, and returns the cosine similarity used to validate the deterministic stub vectors.

symbols.does: Formats a datetime as an ISO string.
symbols.description: iso(dt) returns dt.isoformat(), a small helper used to stamp equal timestamps on the two test annotations so that recency and proximity are controlled variables.
